title: ISE iPSK / MAB / 802.1X Mode Distribution by SSID and Mixed-Mode Anomaly Detection

controlTest.positiveScenario: Add a MAB authorization rule to a corporate 802.1X SSID. UC-17.1.74 detects mixed mode.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Authentication* — events with SSID and AuthenticationMethod. Field: `ssid`, `AuthenticationMethod` (PSK, iPSK, MAB, EAP-TLS, PEAP, EAP-FAST, EAP-TEAP), `authentication_protocol`.
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=5200
| stats count by ssid, AuthenticationMethod
| eventstats sum(count) as total by ssid
| eval pct=round(count/total*100, 2)
| where pct < 100 AND pct > 0
description: Tracks the distribution of authentication modes per SSID. Detects unexpected mixed-mode usage — e.g., a corporate SSID accepting both 802.1X and MAB (MAB is weaker; should not coexist on a strong-auth SSID), or a guest SSID seeing certificate-based auth (suggests misconfigured supplicant).
value: Mixed-mode SSID config is a common security regression — typically the result of operations staff adding MAB exceptions to handle problem devices. Tracking distribution exposes drift and lets the security team enforce policy.
implementation: Schedule the search every 1 hour over the last 4 hours; trigger on count > 0; throttle on `wlc_name`/`ssid` for 1 hour.
visualization: Per-SSID auth-mode pie; per-SSID mode trend over 30d.
knownFalsePositives:
(a) Intentional dual-mode SSIDs (legacy device support) — exclude in `ise_dualmode_ssid_exceptions.csv`.
(b) Test/lab SSIDs.
mitreAttack: N/A (wireless integration — measures WLC↔ISE RADIUS pipeline health, no adversary technique)
grandmaExplanation: Different wifi networks should use the right kind of login. Corporate wifi should require strong proof; guest wifi should be simpler. We watch for mixed setups that weaken security on important networks.
